text.content:
Running This Chapter

Chapter 1 is a sandbox. The characters can explore Ten-Towns in any order. Follow these steps to begin:

  1. Read this overview and the Ten-Towns Overview page below.
  2. Determine the starting town — roll on the @UUID[Compendium.rotf.rotf-tables.RollTable.RotFCh1StartingTown01]{Starting Town table} or choose one.
  3. Once players have their characters and secrets, kick off the adventure with the Cold Open below.
  4. Give the party a starting quest: Cold-Hearted Killer, Nature Spirits, or the quest tied to their starting town.
  5. Whenever characters arrive at a new town for the first time, give them that settlement's quest.

Character Advancement

  • Reach 2nd level after completing their first quest.
  • Reach 3rd level after completing three quests.
  • Reach 4th level after completing five quests.

At 4th level, completing more quests improves the party's reputation rather than granting levels.

Reputation in Ten-Towns

  • At 3rd level: Earn respect from Ten-Towners who know of the party's exploits.
  • At 4th level: Hero's welcome in towns helped; cold shoulder in towns ignored. New adventure opportunities open up in Chapter 2.

Roll on the @UUID[Compendium.rotf.rotf-tables.RollTable.RotFCh1TenTownRumors]{Ten-Towns Rumors table} whenever the characters complete a quest and need direction to the next town.

Ten-Towns Overview

Ten-Towns began four centuries ago as a modest trade post where Bryn Shander now stands. Settlements sprung up on the shores of three lakes: Maer Dualdon, Lac Dinneshere, and Redwaters.

The Three Lakes

  • Maer Dualdon — Deepest lake, largest knucklehead trout population. Towns: Bremen, Lonelywood, Targos, Termalaine.
  • Redwaters — Shallowest, almost completely freezes in winter. Towns: Good Mead, Dougan's Hole. Bryn Shander sits inland on a hill.
  • Lac Dinneshere — Worst winds off the Reghed Glacier. Towns: Caer-Konig, Caer-Dineval, Easthaven.

Snowflake Ratings

Each town is rated on three qualities (❄ = poor, ❄❄❄ = excellent):

  • Friendliness — How welcoming residents are to strangers.
  • Services — Availability of goods, lodging, and spellcasting.
  • Comfort — Quality of food, drink, and accommodation.

Winter Survival Gear

ItemCostWeight
Cold weather clothing10 gp5 lb.
Crampons (2)2 gp¼ lb.
Snowshoes2 gp4 lb.
Sled (empty)20 gp300 lb.
Sled dog50 gp—
Axe beak (domesticated)50 gp—

Sacrifices to Auril

On nights of the new moon, each town makes a sacrifice to appease the Frostmaiden:

  • Humanoid (Bryn Shander, Easthaven, Targos) — Lottery; the unlucky soul is stripped and sent into the tundra to die.
  • Food (Bremen, Caer-Dineval, Caer-Konig, Lonelywood, Termalaine) — A day's catch of knucklehead trout strung up a mile outside town.
  • Warmth (Dougan's Hole, Good Mead) — No fires from dusk to dawn; townsfolk share body heat.

Cold Open

Read the following to players when beginning the adventure:

Icewind Dale has become trapped in a perpetual winter. Ferocious blizzards make the mountain pass through the Spine of the World exceedingly treacherous, and this land has not felt the warmth of the sun in over two years. In fact, the sun no longer appears above the mountains, not even in what should be the height of summer. In this frozen tundra, darkness and bitter cold reign as king and queen. Most dale residents blame Auril the Frostmaiden, the god of winter's wrath. The shimmering aurora that weaves across the sky each night is said to be her doing — a potent spell that keeps the sun at bay.

Dalefolk live in a scattering of settlements known as Ten-Towns. The drop-off in caravans coming from the south and travel between settlements in this never-ending winter has left everyone feeling isolated. Although each town has resolved to appease the Frostmaiden with sacrifices of one kind or another, no respite from winter's fury seems forthcoming. For adventurers such as yourselves, Ten-Towns is a place to test one's mettle and, in the spirit of heroes who have come before, leave one's mark on this frigid, blighted land.

Starting Quests

Cold-Hearted Killer

The characters receive this quest from Hlin Trollbane, a retired bounty hunter (neutral good shield dwarf, unarmored veteran). She hires the party to apprehend and dispose of @UUID[Compendium.rotf.rotf-actors.Actor.SephekKaltro0001]{Sephek Kaltro}, a serial killer in service to Auril.

What Hlin knows:

  • Sephek works for a traveling merchant company called Torg's, owned by Torrga Icevein (dwarf bandit captain).
  • His victims come only from towns that sacrifice people to Auril: Bryn Shander, Easthaven, and Targos.
  • He wears no cold weather clothing and seems immune to the cold.

Reward: 100 gp on proof of Sephek's death.

Sephek Kaltro is a possessed vessel — a mariner whose body was taken over by a frost druid spirit in service to Auril. He has cold immunity, regenerates in cold temperatures, wields conjured ice weapons, and can cast misty step three times per day.

Nature Spirits

The characters receive this quest from Dannika Graysteel (lawful neutral half-elf acolyte), a scholar who believes @UUID[Compendium.rotf.rotf-actors.Actor.ChwingaActorROTF]{chwingas} may hold the key to improving Icewind Dale's climate.

Task: Locate and capture a chwinga, or convince one to help. Dannika provides a lantern of tracking (elementals).

Finding Chwingas: None in the starting town. Each subsequent town visited has a cumulative 25% chance of having chwingas. When found, 1d3 chwingas are playing in a clearing nearby.

Reward: 25 gp + the lantern to keep. Bonus 25 gp if the chwinga was not harmed.

Ten-Towns at a Glance

Individual town journals contain full NPC details, location descriptions, and quest walkthroughs.
